task_completion.description: The bcWebCam application is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the bcWebCam application window visible on the desktop.

task_completion.description: A QR code is visible in the bcWebCam camera feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a QR code image displayed inside the main camera view area of the bcWebCam interface.

task_completion.description: The QR code is successfully decoded by bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a decoded string (a tracking URL) appearing in the scan result or barcode content display area of the bcWebCam application.

task_completion.description: A text editor is open to save the decoded result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor application (such as Notepad) open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The decoded barcode content is pasted into the text file and saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact decoded URL from bcWebCam pasted into the text editor. Check the editor's title bar, 'Save As' dialog, or file explorer to verify the file is named 'scan_result.txt' and is located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'.

integrity.description: The agent used the bcWebCam GUI to decode the QR code, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that there are no terminal windows (Command Prompt, PowerShell) open using command-line tools or scripts to decode the QR code image directly.

integrity.description: The text in the output file matches the decoded text from the bcWebCam GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the string in the text editor exactly matches the string shown in the bcWebCam result area, indicating it was genuinely copied from the application.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to create and save the file, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file was created and edited using a GUI application (like Notepad) rather than using terminal commands like 'echo' or 'Out-File' in PowerShell.
